subject: switch cross-section — Durock T1 nylon/nylon housing, POM stem with a sharp tactile leg high in the stroke
notes: MX-style single-switch cross-section, three exploded tiers: (A) top housing — nylon, trapezoid shell with cruciform stem aperture and LED notch; (B) POM stem with fixed crossbar stubs anchored to the housing inner walls — the stem's flanks carry a hard right-angle shoulder step at y 312-336, positioned high in the stem's full travel span (y 300-460), rather than a rounded mid-travel bulge, to depict the T1's sharp and early-firing tactile bump; (C) bottom housing — nylon, with central spring well, 9-coil spring, and PCB pin contacts. Muted-terracotta splash applied as a soft wash across the shoulder/crossbar contact zone plus a filled accent dot+ring at the right shoulder contact point (652, 324) and a smaller unfilled mirrored tick at the left shoulder (548, 324). Splash value oklch(0.58 0.115 38) chosen to sit between the two terracottas already in rotation — drop-holy-panda-x-deep-dive (0.55 0.105 40) and magnetic-switches-deep-dive (0.62 0.10 45) — with a touch more chroma, reserved for the T1's harder-edged, heavier character; kept distinct from the dusty rose used on topre-electrocapacitive-deep-dive and the ochre used on gateron-lanes-tactile-deep-dive / clicky-switches-deep-dive so the switch-deep-dive rotation stays visually varied. Micro IBM Plex Sans labels (TOP HOUSING · NYLON, STEM · POM, TACTILE LEG, BOTTOM HOUSING · NYLON) with one leader line at reduced opacity. JetBrains Mono technical callout at bottom margin. Brass-bronze ornamental dot at top-right (1148, 92) and left-margin ruler ticks at tier midpoints per family convention.
